name: Low-dose colchicine reduced cardiovascular events in chronic coronary disease
summary: LoDoCo2 supports anti-inflammatory treatment as clinically relevant in chronic atherosclerotic disease.
statement: In chronic coronary disease, 0.5 mg daily colchicine significantly lowered a composite cardiovascular event endpoint compared with placebo.
quantitative_note: Primary endpoint events occurred in 6.8% with colchicine versus 9.6% with placebo, HR 0.69 (95% CI 0.57 to 0.83; P<0.001).
caveats: This is cardiovascular secondary prevention, not direct evidence of slowed biological aging.
Death from noncardiovascular causes was numerically higher with colchicine, HR 1.51 (95% CI 0.99 to 2.31).
Colchicine is broad anti-inflammatory therapy and should not be equated with selective cytokine blockade.
